medical_history.chief_complaint: Loss of conciousness, bi-temporal hemianopsia with total blindness to right eye
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: 10 days prior to presentation, she developed an abrupt onset severe headache, was nauseated, vomited, and had photophobia. Symptoms did not improve after 3 days so she visited her PCP, took Motrin 800mg, and experienced a surge of pain followed by loss of consciousness while driving to the pharmacy. Found to have SAH upon arriving at an OSH and transferred for further evaluation. Initial vision loss in right eye and blurry vision in left eye noted in ED.
medical_history.past_medical_history: s/p cholecystectomy, common bile duct exploration

physical_examination.general_examination: O: T: 98.9 BP: 138/79 HR: 10 R 14 O2 Sats 100% RA. Gen: WD/WN, comfortable, NAD. Pupils: 1.5mm bilaterally EOMI initially, then CN III partial palsy on L. Mild nuchal rigidity.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neuro: Awake and alert, cooperative with exam, normal affect, oriented to person, place, and date. R eye total blindness, L eye temporal hemianopsia, right pupil non reactive. Motor: Normal bulk and tone bilaterally, full power throughout. Sensation: Intact to light touch. Reflexes: B T Br Pa Ac Right 2 1 Left 2 1 Toes downgoing bilaterally.
